summary: Fair‑mix soundboard capture of a solid ’73 show with standout China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, Eyes of the World and They Love Each Other.
review: The performance is a respectable snapshot of September 1973, highlighted by an effortless China Cat > Rider jam, a soaring Eyes of the World, and strong renditions of They Love Each Other and Mississippi Half‑Step. The soundboard source suffers from a poor mix, volume/balance issues and occasional clipping, making parts of the set sound thin. Overall the music outweighs the technical flaws, earning a mixed but generally positive impression.

ai_rating.rationale: Review praises many songs and overall show quality while noting significant recording mix problems; weighted average yields a mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Mix is uneven with volume/balance issues, early set sections sound thin, China Cat Sunflower intro clipped (4‑10 s), and matrix sources require pitch correction. Overall usable but not a high‑fidelity release.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set serves as a warm‑up with a few flat moments but builds momentum; the second set lifts off with strong jams—especially Eyes of the World and the China Cat > Rider sequence—though it is hampered by mixed recording quality in places.
